sanskrit:
अनेकवक्त्रनयनमनेकाद्भुतदर्शनम्।
अनेकदिव्याभरणं दिव्यानेकोद्यतायुधम्॥
दिव्यमाल्याम्बरधरं दिव्यगन्धानुलेपनम्।
सर्वाश्चर्यमयं देवमनन्तं विश्वतोमुखम्॥
transliteration: aneka-vaktra-nayanam anekādbhuta-darśanam / aneka-divyābharaṇaṃ divyānekodyatāyudham // divya-mālyāmbara-dharaṃ divya-gandhānulepanam / sarvāścarya-mayaṃ devam anantaṃ viśvato-mukham //
literal_meaning: many mouths and eyes / many wondrous sights / many divine ornaments / many divine upraised weapons / wearing divine garlands and garments / anointed with divine fragrances / all-wonderful / unlimited / facing every direction
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s ego-bound perception shatters as he confronts the archetype of the Self: an omnidirectional, polyphonic, infinitely armed presence that transcends linear cognition. The vision is a psychic mirror, reflecting back every disowned, idealized, and feared aspect of his inner world in a single luminous gestalt.
psychological_state: Peak transpersonal experience: ego–Self axis temporarily aligned, producing simultaneous expansion dread and sacred attraction.
trigger_scenario: Standing before a vast night-sky image from the James Webb telescope and suddenly grasping that every point of light is an entire galaxy—your personal story shrinks to a speck, yet you feel held inside something intelligent and incomprehensibly large.
anchor_text: See every face as your own and every weapon as your fear.
